title: Planter Box with Attached Trellis
summary: A cedar planter box with a lattice-slat trellis rising from the back, built on a 2x6 base with mitered 2x4 top trim.
description:
A freestanding trellis needs its own footing in the ground; this one skips that problem entirely by rising directly out of the back of a planter box, so the box's own weight and base are what keep the whole structure upright.

The planter is cedar board over a 2x6 base, capped with **mitered 2x4 trim** around the top edge — trim that both hides the plywood-free board-and-batten construction underneath and gives the rim enough thickness to look substantial rather than thin.

**Drill drainage holes in the bottom boards before you plant anything.** It's a five-minute step that's easy to forget once the box is fully assembled and looks finished.

steps.title: Build the planter walls
steps.body: Cut the twelve cedar side boards and the 1x2 connector pieces to length, then assemble two side panels and a front/back panel from them.

steps.title: Join the walls and add the bottom
steps.body: Join all four planter walls together, then cut and attach the two cedar bottom boards, drilling drainage holes before you're done.

steps.title: Build and attach the base
steps.body: Cut the 2x6 base rails and attach the assembled planter box on top of them.

steps.title: Add the mitered top trim
steps.body: Cut and miter the 2x4 top trim pieces at 45 degrees and attach them around the top edge of the planter.

steps.title: Build and attach the trellis
steps.body: Cut the 2x2 trellis uprights and cross braces and assemble the lattice trellis frame with the 1x2 slats, then attach the finished trellis to the back of the planter.
